frame_analyses.description: Title slide showing 'Laparoscopic Roaders Knot' by Prof. Dr. R. K. Mishra
Hands demonstrating suture handling with white suture material
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean suture handling technique, appropriate grip on suture ends

frame_analyses.description: Formation of first extracorporeal throw with bilateral hand manipulation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth bilateral coordination, proper loop formation technique

frame_analyses.description: Sequential throw formation with maintained tension
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Consistent throw size and tension control

frame_analyses.description: Final throw completion showing properly formed knot structure
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Well-formed knot with appropriate loop spacing

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher being positioned for advancement
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper knot pusher orientation and approach angle

frame_analyses.description: Knot advancement with pusher showing controlled delivery
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth pusher advancement with maintained knot integrity

knot_formation.notes: Clean extracorporeal knot formation with appropriate throw sequence

confidence_rationale: Clear demonstration of knot tying technique visible throughout. Cannot assess target placement, slit closure, or tissue interaction as this appears to be a pure knot demonstration without Penrose drain.
cannot_determine: Suture placement through marked targets - no Penrose drain visible
Slit closure assessment - no tissue substrate shown
Final knot security under tension - no tension testing demonstrated
Suture cutting phase - demonstration ends before cutting
technique_summary: Expert-level demonstration of extracorporeal knot formation with excellent bilateral hand coordination and proper knot pusher technique. Clean, efficient movement pattern typical of experienced surgeon.
strengths: Smooth bilateral hand coordination during throw formation
Proper knot pusher positioning and advancement technique

demonstrated_techniques: Proper knot pusher positioning and advancement
common_errors_addressed: Maintaining proper loop spacing during throw formation
Avoiding knot deformation during pusher advancement

improvement_suggestions: Video shows isolated knot technique but lacks context of full FLS Task 4 requirements
Would benefit from demonstration of suture placement and tissue interaction phases
